surfaces.note: All surfaces are very dark purple-near-black. input (#221E34) has the highest luminance and is therefore the most challenging surface for accent contrast.

text.primary.wcag: AA 4.5:1 normal text — all surfaces pass with very high margin
text.primary.note: Soft warm white with purple undertone — reads as white against dark surfaces while harmonizing with the purple-tinted surface palette.

text.secondary.wcag: AA 4.5:1 normal text — all surfaces pass with strong margin
text.secondary.note: Hot pink-family secondary text — model names and thread titles carry energy. Lighter than borders.default (#FF2070) so it reads as clearly readable text rather than chrome. This color gives Outrun its synthwave character at the text level.

text.muted.wcag: AA 4.5:1 normal text — all surfaces pass
text.muted.note: Muted purple — timestamps and placeholder text. Desaturated relative to the surface undertone, reads as dim without being inaccessible.

text.inverse.wcag: AA 4.5:1 — passes on all accent-colored button and badge surfaces

borders.default.wcag: borders.default is a structural border, not text. WCAG SC 1.4.3 applies to text and images of text — not to decorative structural borders. The 4.37:1 on input is below the 4.5:1 text threshold but above the 3:1 large-text threshold. Card borders do not appear on input backgrounds in normal layout. All other surfaces pass the full 4.5:1 threshold.
borders.default.note: Hot pink neon card borders are the dominant visual character statement of Outrun v2. Intentional — consistent with the Outrun creative brief.

borders.subtle.wcag: Exempt — decorative structural separator, not text or interactive control. Near-invisible hairline divider. Consistent with all other themes where borders.subtle is intentionally low-contrast.

borders.strong.wcag: AA — all surfaces pass with very high margin
borders.strong.note: Night Drive teal — the teal counterpoint to hot pink. Used on focused input outlines and emphasized borders.

interactive.focusRing.ratios: Same as borders.strong — 9.91:1 worst case (on input). AA PASS.
interactive.focusRing.note: Teal focus ring is clearly visible on all surfaces and distinct from text content.

accents.model-claude.wcag: AA — all surfaces pass. Amber identity unchanged — holds perfectly on purple-tinted dark surfaces.

accents.model-gpt.wcag: AA — all surfaces pass. GPT shifts from pure cyan (#00FFFF) to Night Drive teal (#2EE4B9) — more sophisticated, clearly teal family. Consistent with the Night Drive palette where teal is the secondary accent.

accents.model-gemini.wcag: AA — all surfaces pass. Neon purple, brightened from #D040FF to #D060FF (+32 green channel) to maintain contrast on the new purple-tinted surfaces.

accents.model-other.wcag: AA — all surfaces pass. Warm peach/coral from Night Drive palette. Visually distinct from the hot-pink chrome (#FF2070, #FF6EB4).

accents.model-grok.wcag: AA — all surfaces pass with very high margin. Light periwinkle — distinct from GPT teal (#2EE4B9) and DeepSeek blue (#7AA0FF).

accents.model-deepseek.wcag: AA — all surfaces pass. Electric periwinkle-blue — distinct from GPT teal and Grok light periwinkle.

accents.model-mistral.wcag: AA — all surfaces pass. Coral pink — in the pink family but warmer/lighter than the dominant hot pink (#FF2070). Mistral identity preserved.

semantic.success.wcag: AA — all surfaces pass. Mint green — clearly green rather than teal, distinguishable from model-gpt (#2EE4B9) and borders.strong (#2EE4B9).

semantic.warning.wcag: AA — all surfaces pass. Warm yellow.

semantic.error.wcag: AA — all surfaces pass. Pure red — unambiguously an error state, not confused with the pink hot-pink family.

semantic.info.wcag: AA — all surfaces pass. Night Drive purple (#CD44FF) — 4.50:1 on input is the exact AA threshold (meets requirement). Used for streaming/loading state indicators.
semantic.info.note: Thinnest margin in the Outrun v2 audit. If semantic.info is ever needed as body text on the input surface in a future use case, bump to #D060FF (5.22:1 worst case). For the current use case (small streaming indicator icon/label), 4.50:1 is sufficient.

_design_notes.revision: Issue #96 v2 — complete palette direction change from navy/electric-blue (take 1) to dark-purple-near-black / hot-pink / teal synthwave. Inspiration: Night Drive VS Code theme palette (mood board, not 1:1 mapping).
_design_notes.surface_philosophy: All surfaces are very dark purple-near-black — not pure black, not navy. The purple undertone (#16141D / #1E1B2E / #120F1A / #221E34) is the essential character shift from take 1, which used navy-blue surfaces. It reads as moody and nocturnal rather than technical. Directly references Night Drive's #16141d base. Progressive lightening: sidebar < background < card < input.
_design_notes.hot_pink_as_dominant_accent: Hot pink (#FF2070) is the primary structural accent — card borders are the largest-area chrome element. text.secondary (#FF6EB4) extends the pink family into readable text. interactive.hover/active use purple tints rather than pink to avoid pink-on-pink hover wash. The send button and other filled interactive elements use hot pink with dark-surface inverse text.
_design_notes.teal_as_counterpoint: Teal (#2EE4B9, Night Drive's secondary accent) is the counterpoint to pink: borders.strong, focusRing, model-gpt, and semantic.success (mint green #3DDB96 to distinguish from exact teal). The hot-pink/teal pairing is the chromatic engine of Outrun v2. This is the Synthwave 84 / outrun racing aesthetic — magenta and cyan neon against dark sky.
_design_notes.shadow_philosophy: Shadows use rgba pink and teal glow channels. shadow.sm is a pure pink corona — subtle lift with neon glow character. shadow.md layers a pink primary glow with black depth. shadow.lg creates a multi-hue neon corona: pink primary (40% opacity), teal secondary (20% opacity), black depth — appropriate for high-elevation elements like the model selector panel.
_design_notes.model_identity_shifts: Claude amber (#FFE600) unchanged. GPT shifts from pure cyan (#00FFFF) to Night Drive teal (#2EE4B9) — same family, more sophisticated. Gemini brightened to #D060FF to compensate for the modestly higher luminance of the purple-tinted surfaces versus the former near-black surfaces. Grok is light periwinkle (#C0CFFF) — high contrast, ethereal. DeepSeek is electric periwinkle (#7AA0FF). Mistral is coral pink (#FF6090) — pink family but distinct from chrome. model-other shifts from pure magenta (#FF00AA) to warm peach (#FF8D77) — coral-warm, clearly different from the hot-pink chrome.
_design_notes.night_drive_treatment: Night Drive is a code editor theme — the mapping is intentionally non-literal. The moody dark-purple-near-black base and the hot-pink/teal pairing are taken as the essential mood. Code editor accent colors (keyword purple, string teal, constant peach) inform the model accent and semantic palettes. The cursor hot pink (#FF1A66 in Night Drive, #FF2070 here — slightly brighter to pass card surface contrast) becomes the dominant UI accent.
